practice: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations
notes: Single fail-closed egress path in src/lib/ai/egress/; MAC ceiling (FOUO+) withheld regardless of tenant class; EgressDecision logged per turn; arch test enforces single-path. CUI-by-reference (opaque-handle resolver, src/lib/ai/egress/handles.ts): when a CUI field is WITHHELD from the CUI-blind model, an unguessable conversation-scoped token (h:<base64url(18 random bytes)>, 144 bits) is minted for the value (sealed at rest in egress_handles via the vault, never plaintext) and the TOKEN — not the value — is surfaced to the model, so the agent can MOVE/FILE/ROUTE CUI it never reads. Tokens RESOLVE to the real value IN-BOUNDARY (before the executor runs) only within the conversation they were minted in (resolveHandle verifies conversationId — a token from conversation A does NOT resolve in B; the cross-conversation/cross-tenant boundary); the executor result is re-gated by the same chokepoint (no echo-back to the model). WRITE-PATH TAINT (v2.13.0, src/lib/ai/agent-loop.ts): each handle is bound at mint to the effective ceiling of the data it stands for, and that mint-ceiling is enforced on BOTH flow directions — (a) the read-back gate folds it (max-by-rank) into the resolving turn's result ceiling, and (b) the persist path REJECTS fail-closed BEFORE executeTool any tool call that resolves a handle minted at ceiling X into a target context (destination project's, or org's, effective ceiling) classified BELOW X. The CUI is never written into the lower-classification container, never reaches the model (the rejection names only the classification LEVELS, never the value), and the prevented down-classification write is logged as decidedBy handle_taint_block (counts + content-hash of the ORIGINAL handle args, never the resolved CUI). This closes the residual where the agent could launder CUI DOWN by filing a withheld value into an entity cleared only for a lower level. Both events are recorded to the egress_decisions trail (decidedBy handle_mint / handle_resolve / handle_taint_block, counts + content-hash, never CUI). Default-deny minting (HANDLEABLE_FIELDS allowlist only); EXPOSABLE_FIELDS read-allowlist unchanged; gated by EGRESS_HANDLES_ENABLED (off = fields dropped + no resolution + no taint check, as before). Threat-model covered by handles.test.ts + augment.test.ts + handles-loop.redteam.test.ts (cross-conversation isolation, no-echo-back, exact-match, default-deny, flag-off parity) + write-path-taint.redteam.test.ts (down-classification BLOCK with executor-not-called, at-or-above ALLOW, no-handle unaffected, no-projectId=org-ceiling, flag-off parity). GOV EXPOSABILITY-MAP SIGN-OFF GATE (cutover finisher, scripts/cutover/lib/exposability.ts + orchestrate.mjs): the field-level default-deny exposability map (what the CUI-blind model may SEE per tool result — projection.ts EXPOSABLE_FIELDS/HANDLEABLE_FIELDS/TOOL_ENTITY merged with the connector registry) is a script-authored AC-4 flow-control authorization. Before a GOV tenant's cutover flip is allowed, that EXACT map is captured as a canonical, hash-stable snapshot (exposability-snapshot.mjs — JSON + reviewer markdown + sha256, assembled from the LIVE merged maps the gate enforces, never a re-hardcoded copy) and a named reviewer signs off on it (compliance/exposability/signoff/<orgSlug>.json = {orgSlug, mapHash, reviewer, signedAt, leakTestPassed}). The orchestrator's requireExposabilitySignoff enforces, fail-closed, that a GOV flip proceeds ONLY with a sign-off whose mapHash equals the CURRENT map hash AND leakTestPassed===true — a missing file, a stale hash (the map changed since sign-off), or a failed leak test BLOCKS the flip before any freeze; commercial flips need no sign-off (unaffected). So an authorization for CUI flow to the commercial model is a human-reviewed, hash-bound, leak-tested approval that can never silently diverge from the running gate. RUNTIME-CONFIG GOV DESIGNATION (design §8): the per-org tenantClass that selects gov default-deny vs commercial flow is a PLATFORM-OWNER authority (isInternalAdmin), separated from the tenant-admin connector-enablement authority; a flip to GOV atomically applies the gov guardrails (breadth/MCP off, commercial-only connectors stripped) and the tenant-admin runtime-config PATCH cannot lift them (server-side 400) — so the information-flow class itself cannot be widened by a tenant. See src/lib/runtime-config/guardrails.ts + the tenant-class/runtime-config routes.

practice: Verify and control/limit connections to external systems
notes: SSRF guard on outbound webhook URLs (src/lib/security/webhook-url.ts); external connections to commercial SaaS are brokered through a single CONTROLLED broker — self-hosted Nango (OSS unified-API/OAuth engine) running IN-BOUNDARY on the compose network (the `nango` profile: cosmos-nango-server + dedicated postgres + redis, all digest-pinned; the app reaches it ONLY at the internal NANGO_HOST, never the public internet directly). Nango holds the per-provider OAuth/API-key credentials org-scoped; the app proxies data through it and NEVER handles the provider token (CA-3 system-interconnection / SA-9 external-service controlled via a broker). NANGO_SECRET_KEY is a platform secret injected via env/Docker-secret, never logged, never returned to client/model. D5 COMMERCIAL-ONLY gov-block (load-bearing): a gov tenant can NEVER reach Nango by ANY path — defense in depth at 4 layers: (L1) the connector registry's tenant-filtered tool list excludes the commercial-only Nango descriptor for tenantClass=gov so the CUI-blind model never SEES a nango_* tool (src/lib/ai/connectors/registry.ts connectorToolDefs(tenantClass) + agent-loop.ts); (L2) executeConnectorTool hard-REFUSES a commercial-only tool for a gov (or class-absent → fail-closed) ctx, audited as decidedBy connector_availability_block; (L3) the Nango executor hard-checks tenantClass!==commercial at the TOP before any broker call (src/lib/ai/executors/nango.ts); (L4) the connect-session route returns 403 for any non-COMMERCIAL org, audited, before any Nango call (src/app/api/v1/orgs/[orgId]/integrations/nango/connect/route.ts). Org default tenantClass is GOV (fail-closed). Nango tool results have NO egress entity mapping ⇒ full-withhold on the gov/withheld path (moot — blocked); commercial flows below FOUO with the marking-DLP tripwire still applied. Proven by src/lib/ai/connectors/gov-block.test.ts (L1/L2 + egress full-withhold), src/lib/ai/executors/nango.test.ts (L3), the connect route test (L4), and the invariant lock (a gov tenant's connector tool list is byte-identical to pre-Nango).

AU-11 sanctioned RETENTION-PURGE now SHIPPED (migration 20260606130000_audit_retention_checkpoint + scripts/dsop/purge-audit.mjs): the ONLY legitimate audit-delete path. Owner-only (refuses cosmos_app which can neither DELETE nor SET session_replication_role); records a signed chain-checkpoint at the purge boundary then DELETEs rows<=N in ONE owner txn (SET LOCAL session_replication_role=replica - the only trigger-bypass
column8: for that txn only). Hard guards: refuses below the 1095-day gov floor unless an explicit TEST-only AUDIT_PURGE_ALLOW_BELOW_FLOOR_DAYS override; refuses if N>latest WORM-attested toSeq (WORM-export-FIRST ordering so purged rows are anchored offsite first); idempotent. verify_audit_chain re-anchors at the checkpoint (first retained row prev_hash==checkpoint_row_hash) so the chain stays verifiable across the purge boundary - cosmos_app STILL cannot delete audit rows (REVOKE+trigger unchanged). Empirically proven on PG16. See docs/runbooks/audit-integrity.md sec 4

practice: Protect audit information and audit tools from unauthorized access; modification; and deletion
notes: DB trigger blocks UPDATE/DELETE on audit_logs and egress_decisions; REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE from cosmos_app role; only postgres superuser retains schema-modification rights. Tamper-EVIDENCE added: audit-worm-export.mjs ships audit_logs+egress_decisions (each as an independent per-table export stream/watermark) to the object-locked cosmos-audit-worm bucket (MinIO COMPLIANCE object-lock + append-only key: write + read for verification; NO delete/overwrite/retention-bypass) with a per-table signed sha256 manifest — an offsite immutable anchor that detects post-hoc DB tampering by diff. Exporter connects read-only as cosmos_app (SELECT). In-DB per-row hash-chain now SHIPPED (migration 20260606070000_audit_hash_chain): every audit_logs+egress_decisions row carries a sha256 row_hash binding the prior row's hash + this row's length-framed columns (pgcrypto digest; metadata framed as text never jsonb_each; all-bytes-safe length-prefixed bytea so no NUL separator); a BEFORE INSERT trigger fills it under a per-table advisory lock (no forks - empirically proven on PG16 with 200 concurrent inserts: 0 forks 1 genesis 1 tail all reachable). verify_audit_chain(regclass) + scripts/dsop/verify-audit-chain.mjs (cosmos_app SELECT-only ops one-shot exits non-zero on a break) detect content edits forged/inserted rows mid-chain deletions and missing/duplicate genesis - in-DB tamper-EVIDENCE complementing the offsite WORM anchor (defense in depth). AU-11 RETENTION-PURGE checkpoint now SHIPPED (migration 20260606130000): verify_audit_chain is checkpoint-aware (anchors at genesis OR at the latest signed checkpoint's row_hash once the genesis was purged) and the audit_chain_checkpoint table is append-only for the app (cosmos_app SELECT-only; INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE REVOKEd). The checkpoint HMAC sig is verified in scripts/dsop/verify-audit-chain.mjs (the SQL fn anchors structurally; the JS wrapper recomputes HMAC(table||seq||hex(rowHash)) constant-time and exits 2 on a forged/unverifiable checkpoint) - so a forged checkpoint that re-anchors a tampered chain is still caught. See docs/runbooks/audit-integrity.md sec 4 (the retention-purge checkpoint procedure)

Stored credential-class secrets — per-tenant OIDC client secrets AND external connector credentials (Google OAuth refresh tokens via connector_credentials.secret_enc; src/lib/integrations/credentials.ts) — are sealed at rest with the AES-256-GCM vault keyring (src/lib/crypto/vault.ts) under a rotatable active key — never stored in plaintext — and that key is itself rotatable (3.13.10). The legacy plaintext Google refresh-token column is drained to the sealed store on first use (self-heal) and then nulled. POST-CUTOVER CREDENTIAL HYGIENE (IA-5
column8: cutover finisher scripts/cutover/revoke-google-tokens.mjs): the per-tenant cutover is COPY-not-move
column9: so a migrated Google OAuth refresh token stays LIVE at the provider after the flip (v1 keeps it as a rollback target during soak). Once the flip is permanent

column13: idempotent (an already-revoked token's 400 invalid_token is treated as done)
column14: and the token is opened in-boundary for the one call and NEVER logged (the per-token report keys on user_id only) — so a copied credential cannot be used from the decommissioned v1. A rollback PAST the revoke requires user re-consent (documented in docs/runbooks/cutover.md §7)"

practice: Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations
notes: pgBackRest WAL archiving + base backups to the in-boundary MinIO S3 repo (cosmos-pgbackrest); repo encrypted at rest with AES-256-CBC (repo1-cipher-type) independent of MinIO SSE; tested restore via scripts/dsop/restore-drill.sh (CP-10 — restores to a scratch cluster + verification query asserting audit_logs present); RPO ≤ 5 min (WAL) / RTO ≤ 1 hr documented. Backup repo is in-boundary (GovCloud/Assured-Workloads S3 in prod); single-site today with offsite replication a documented follow-on. Migration-integrity TOOLING (not a control per se) for the v1->v2 per-tenant cutover ships in scripts/cutover/: export-org/import-org (idempotent UPSERT replay; FK-safe owner load; DataClassification dedupe-with-audit; markings carried verbatim) + verify-org (per-model exact counts + PER-ROW money equality + the CUI/FOUO marking invariant + a sampled content checksum; exits non-zero on any mismatch = the hard pre-flip gate) + a source-side write-freeze (frozen_orgs -> proxy 405 on writes). Adjacent to CP-10: it proves a tenant's data (incl. money + CUI markings) is preserved + verifiable across a migration; rollback = re-route + restore the pre-flip snapshot. BUILD-ONLY today (docs/runbooks/cutover.md carries the never-run-vs-prod-without-sign-off banner). Cutover schema-parity + provenance HARD gate now SHIPPED (scripts/cutover/parity-gate.mjs — migration-integrity TOOLING not a control per se; the §9.2 prerequisite that must be green before ANY tenant cutover): it restores a `pg_dump --schema-only` prod snapshot into a throwaway scratch DB (pgcrypto+pgvector ensured) and asserts (1) `prisma migrate diff` between the restored snapshot and v2's REAL schema (prisma/migrations replayed into a throwaway shadow DB via --to-migrations -- NOT the lossy schema.prisma datamodel which omits raw-SQL-only objects: the audit seq IDENTITY/content_tsv GENERATED column/pgvector HNSW indexes/hash-chain trigger objects) is EMPTY = structural parity (a non-empty diff is captured + FAILS) and (2) the FK data_classifications.project_id -> projects.id EXISTS in the snapshot via a dedicated pg_constraint probe = proof the baseline came from the classification-propagation line not an older branch (v2 declares this FK in migration 20260606140000_data_classification_project_fk). It then records provenance to compliance/provenance/prod-baseline.json — prod git commit + a stable sha256 over prod's ordered (_prisma_migrations migration_name+checksum) history + the gate verdict — so the cutover source-of-truth is auditable. Fail-closed: exits non-zero on any error / non-empty diff / missing FK. SYNTHETIC-tested in Docker (two scratch PG: a matching v2-migrated dump PASSes + writes provenance; a dump with the classification FK dropped FAILs part 2; a dump with a table/column dropped FAILs part 1). Run against real prod is a future coordinated step. Near-zero-downtime cutover now SHIPPED (migration-integrity TOOLING not a control per se): scripts/cutover/soak-sync.mjs is an incremental watermark delta replay (per-table watermark = updated_at else created_at else full-scan; advances to the max observed value -> no clock-skew window; reuses the export/import cores; deletes are invisible to a delta BY DESIGN) that keeps v2 caught up while v1 is live so the freeze window is tiny; scripts/cutover/reconcile-org.mjs is the under-freeze FINAL reconcile (final idempotent import + DELETE-EXTRAS by org-scoped PK-set diff for mutable org-owned non-audit tables ONLY -- NEVER append-only/audit (refused by name -- AU-9 immutability) NEVER the referential-closure parents (shared users/globals are not org-owned) NEVER another org's rows -- children-before-parents reverse-FK-topological in one owner txn under session_replication_role=replica + an in-txn orphan probe that ROLLS BACK the whole reconcile on any dangling FK; a delete count over --large-threshold fails closed) then the verify gate. practice: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls
notes: check:control-coverage CI gate monitors SSP completeness continuously; SAST/SCA gates run on every build; full CONMON (continuous monitoring) program not yet formalized. The egress exposability control (AC-4, 3.1.3) is continuously monitorable via its canonical hash-stable snapshot (scripts/cutover/exposability-snapshot.mjs ⇒ a sha256 over the LIVE merged map): any drift in the field-level default-deny map changes the hash, which INVALIDATES (makes stale) every gov tenant's prior exposability sign-off — so the control's effectiveness review is re-triggered on any change to what the model may see. The sign-off's leakTestPassed is bound to the golden-egress + projection-contract leak suites (no CUI/free-text field is ever exposed), which run on every build — tying the gov go-live authorization to a green leak test.

practice: Develop; document; and periodically update system security plans

practice: Employ architectural designs; software development techniques; and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security

The connector accessor (getCredential/setCredential for per-user; getOrgCredential/setOrgCredential for org-level userId-null rows) is the single sealed path.
